commit.message:
Fix typo

Fix grammar error in Basic Workload Identity Scenario.

"is authorized 2" with a number makes no sense in context.

I think the intended meaning is "is authorized to" and that this phrase
needs a verb. The additional comma makes the sentence easier to
understand.

Identify workload by number

Basic Workload Identity Scenario identifies example workloads using numerals not words.

Fix one sentence that used a "workload three" instead of "workload 3".

verification.payload:
tree ab640290595e694054e14f81c14b735df97433bb
parent 586a5d31ab7ee2975c3eaba8d8defabacd05e1b0
author Josh Kline <jkanywhere@users.noreply.github.com> 1766192748 -0800
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1766192748 -0800

Identify workload by number

Basic Workload Identity Scenario identifies example workloads using numerals not words.

Fix one sentence that used a "workload three" instead of "workload 3".

commit.message:
WIT `sub` claim (#202) (#226)

* Move wimse URI scheme to identifier draft; drop duplicate IANA (#207)

Point WIT text and -01 document history at WIMSE-ID; remove local URI
scheme IANA section and IANA.uri-schemes ref.

Made-with: Cursor

* Clarify WIT `sub`: Workload Identifier and granular-auth (issue 202)

Define `sub` as a Workload Identifier from WIMSE-ID; point to
granular-auth for additional requirements, including use for
workload-to-workload traffic.

Made-with: Cursor

---------

Co-authored-by: ysheffer <Yaron_Sheffer@intuit.com>

commit.message:
HTTP-Sig: wimse-aud signature parameter (#221) (#222)

* HTTP-Sig: replace Wimse-Audience with wimse-aud signature param (#221)

- Use wimse-aud as an RFC 9421 signature metadata parameter; IANA §6.3
- Drop separate Wimse-Audience header; cite IANA registry via informative ref
- Update non-normative request example Signature-Input (Signature not regenerated)
- Document history for -03

Made-with: Cursor

* Clarify default wimse-aud and deployment-specific audience text

Made-with: Cursor

* Refresh HTTP signature examples from wimse-s2s-doc; rfcfold .out files

commit.message:
Clarify response signing section (#190)

* Clarify response signing section

- Clarify that Accept-Signature is a preference, not a mandate
- Specify which components to include in Accept-Signature header
- Clarify that client (recipient of response) must validate signatures
- Add requirement to reject responses if signature is absent or invalid

* Apply suggestion from @yaroslavros

Co-authored-by: Yaroslav Rosomakho <yaroslavros@gmail.com>

* Accept-Signature details

---------

Co-authored-by: Yaroslav Rosomakho <yaroslavros@gmail.com>

verification.payload:
tree 7bcf22075dd5581e35e38b90ab23bb11c41d1670
parent 9a4efb36ec89db8700e67788231f9b605aac9ffd
author Yaron Sheffer <yaronf.ietf@gmail.com> 1767545004 +0200
committer GitHub <noreply@github.com> 1767545004 +0200

Clarify response signing section (#190)

* Clarify response signing section

- Clarify that Accept-Signature is a preference, not a mandate
- Specify which components to include in Accept-Signature header
- Clarify that client (recipient of response) must validate signatures
- Add requirement to reject responses if signature is absent or invalid

* Apply suggestion from @yaroslavros

Co-authored-by: Yaroslav Rosomakho <yaroslavros@gmail.com>

* Accept-Signature details

---------

Co-authored-by: Yaroslav Rosomakho <yaroslavros@gmail.com>
